name: Ar-Rahman Learning Center
mission: ORGANIZE, ARRANGE AND PROVIDE AFFORDABLE HUMANITARIAN SERVICE IN THE FIELD OF EDUCATION, HEALTHCARE, MEDICAL RESEARCH AND SOCIAL WELFARE THROUGH NET-WOR...
theoryOfChange: Ar-Rahman Institute (arqi.org) describes a '5-Step Academic System' as part of their approach to help students succeed. This system includes Concept Building, Practice Worksheets, Weekly Tests, Performance Analysis, and One-to-One Mentorship. Their approach is described as structured, concept-driven coaching that combines expert teaching, regular assessments, and personal mentorship to ensure consistent academic growth, aiming for excellence in school exams, board results, and competitive entrances.
scoreSummary: Too early to rate numerically. This organization was founded in 2021 and is shown as an emerging organization while it builds a public track record.

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Early-stage organization. We show qualitative context while it builds a longer public track record.

keyConcerns.headline: Expenses are 2.2x revenue ($396,080 vs $181,050)
keyConcerns.detail: Spending significantly exceeding revenue is unsustainable without reserves drawdown.

keyConcerns.headline: Holds 786 months of reserves without clear endowment model
keyConcerns.detail: Organizations holding >36 months of reserves should publish deployment plans.

judge_issues.message: The rationale mentions exceptional financial efficiency and stability but doesn't fully address the significant risks identified, such as expenses exceeding revenue and the large reserve without a clear endowment model. These risks contributed to a deduction that lowered the overall score.

The impact rationale mentions a strong focus on capacity building and a high program expense ratio, but it doesn't adequately address the financial health concerns stemming from expenses exceeding revenue and the large working capital reserve without a clear plan. This omission makes the rationale incomplete.
Expenses ($396,080) exceed revenue ($181,050) by 119% - may be drawing down reserves
Revenue diverges >80% across sources ($181,050 - $4,559,370) - different fiscal years (1 year spread)
Extremely high working capital (785.8 months) may indicate hoarding or data error

risks.description: Holds 786 months of reserves without clear endowment model

dimension_explanations.alignment: The organization shows a medium fit for donors interested in global education and social welfare, specifically within an Islamic educational context [3].
dimension_explanations.impact: The charity demonstrates a strong focus on capacity building through its educational programs, allocating 84.1% of its $181,050 total revenue directly to its missions [1][3]. While it maintains a massive reserve of 785.8 months of working capital, specific evidence of long-term outcomes or cost-per-beneficiary data is currently unavailable [1].

The organization focuses on community capacity building through its core programs, which include Quran classes and Arabic language studies [3].

strengths_deep_dive: AR-Rahman Learning Center Inc's financial model is distinguished by its lean operational structure. With <cite id="2">$4,559,370 in total revenue</cite> primarily from contributions, the organization manages to maintain <cite id="7">zero fundraising costs</cite>. This efficiency is paired with a diverse programmatic reach that includes <cite id="4">Arabic language and Islamic studies</cite> across multiple international jurisdictions including <cite id="5">Egypt, India, and Australia</cite>. The organization's ability to scale, evidenced by its <cite id="3">4242.3% revenue growth</cite>, indicates a rapidly expanding donor base and increasing demand for its affordable humanitarian services.
rich_narrative.summary: AR-Rahman Learning Center Inc is a New York-based non-profit organization that provides <cite id="1">affordable humanitarian services in education, healthcare, and social welfare</cite>. The organization has experienced rapid financial expansion, reporting <cite id="2">total revenue of $4,559,370</cite> in the 2023 tax year, representing a <cite id="3">3-year revenue CAGR of 4242.3%</cite>. Its primary operational model focuses on capacity building through specialized educational programs, including <cite id="4">Quran classes, Arabic language studies, and Islamic studies</cite> offered in various formats such as private and group sessions. While the organization maintains a global outlook with geographic coverage spanning the <cite id="5">USA, UK, Saudi Arabia, Pakistan, and Indonesia</cite>, its financial structure is characterized by an exceptionally high <cite id="6">program expense ratio of 84.1%</cite> according to recent tax filings. This suggests that all reported expenditures are directed toward its core mission of providing humanitarian and educational services. Furthermore, the organization demonstrates high fundraising efficiency, spending <cite id="7">$0.00 to raise every $1</cite> in contributions. Despite this efficiency, the charity faces sustainability challenges, as its <cite id="8">expenses have significantly exceeded revenue</cite> in recent periods, though it maintains <cite id="9">785.8 months of working capital</cite> in working capital to bridge funding gaps.
